rationale.R1.2: The argument chain (selective gate → robustness → mAP gains → SOTA claim) must be internally consistent; the single ablation point (0.4 mAP drop) is load-bearing for the causal attribution and needs scrutiny for logical sufficiency.
rationale.R1.3: Core responsibility: every quantitative claim (89.2/81.4 mAP, deltas vs CoBEVT and PointPillars, backbone generalization) must be traceable to reported experimental results; the section lives or dies on numeric fidelity.
rationale.R1.4: The SOTA claim over CoBEVT rests on margins of 0.5 and 0.8 mAP—small deltas that require checking whether the comparison is fair (same protocol, same split, same channel model) and whether other recent baselines are omitted.
